provenance.author: Poly by Google
provenance.notes: Coconut palm from Poly by Google, distributed via poly.pizza/m/bXUTyfiwqBb under CC-BY (source labels it 'CC-BY'; normalized to CC-BY-4.0 here, attribution preserved). Normalized via @gltf-transform, then trunk-straightened so the ground contact and crown share one stable vertical centerline for LOD; min.y=0 and the trunk base is at the asset origin. Webp-compressed (2.38MB -> 329KB). Modeled fronds (not alpha planes), so the baked card silhouette is the true palm shape. Distinct kebab id 'coconut-palm' so it never collides with the legacy camelCase 'coconut' billboard namespace. Credit in THIRD-PARTY-ASSETS.md.

lod.label: mesh-near+straight-card-far
lod.rationale: A 27m coconut palm is a sparse coastal landmark. The source mesh is trunk-straightened and grounded so the visible base stays at the instance origin, then it uses the real mesh near the player and a single 1024px-high baked card beyond ~50m. This reverts away from the octahedral impostor path because the curved trunk exposed view-cell snapping and leaf-edge pixelation; the straight centered trunk keeps the crossed far card aligned from orbit angles without expanding the previous 140m outer range.

bands.notes: 1024px-high baked front-view alpha card from scripts/bake-veg-card.mjs after trunk straightening; rendered as a two-sided 2-plane cross so both sides keep the same foliage lighting; instanced and finite-ranged at 140m.
